name: Composite TOP
description: The Composite TOP is a multi-input TOP that will perform a composite operation for each input. Select the composite operation using the Operation parameter on the Composite parameter page.
summary: The Composite TOP is a multi-input TOP that will perform a composite operation for each input. Select the composite operation using the Operation parameter on the Composite parameter page.
tips: Use different operation modes (Over, Add, Multiply, Screen) for various compositing effects.
The 'Over' operation is standard alpha compositing.
Multiple inputs are composited in order - first input is the bottom layer.

parameters.name: TOP
parameters.label: TOP
parameters.description: In addition to all the inputs attached, you can specify more using the TOPs listed in this field.

parameters.name: Preview Grid
parameters.label: Preview Grid
parameters.description: previewgrid - This outputs an image showing the effect of all operation types in a grid, with the inputs swapped on the right side of each tile.

parameters.name: Select Input
parameters.label: Select Input
parameters.description: selectinput - Instead of doing the composites, this causes only one of the inputs to pass through.

parameters.name: Input Index
parameters.label: Input Index
parameters.description: inputindex - When passing through an input with Select Input on, this is the index of the image that is passed through.

parameters.name: Operation
parameters.label: Operation
parameters.description: Operation parameter.

parameters.name: Add
parameters.label: Add
parameters.description: (input1.rgba * input2.a) + (input2.rgba * (1.0 - input1.a)) average - (input1.rgba + input2.rgba)/2 brightest - burncolor - burnlinear - chromadifference - color - darkercolor - difference - absoluteValue(input1.rgb - input2.rgb). Alpha always equals 1.0 dimmest - divide - input1.rgba  / input2.rgba dodge - exclude - freeze - glow - hardlight - hardmix - heat - hue - inside - input1.rgba * clamp(input2.a, 0.0, 1.0) insideluminance - inverse - lightercolor - luminancedifference - maximum - max(in

parameters.name: Swap Operation Order
parameters.label: Swap Operation Order
parameters.description: swaporder - Swaps the order of the input pairs. A operation B is changed to B operation A. Operations like Add don't matter, but many do, like Over and Hard Light.

parameters.name: Fixed Layer
parameters.label: Fixed Layer
parameters.description:
size -  - The selected input will become the fixed layer and the other input will be the overlay. This does not change the order of the composite (Input1 + Input2), only which layer is considered fixed and which layer is adjustable by the parameters on the Transform page. The resolution and aspect ratio of the Fixed Layer is used as the composite's final resolution and aspect ratio unless manually on the Common Page

 input1 - input2 -

parameters.name: Input 1
parameters.label: Input 1
parameters.description: input1 - input2 -

parameters.name: Pre-Fit Overlay
parameters.label: Pre-Fit Overlay
parameters.description: Pre-Fit Overlay parameter.

parameters.name: Fill
parameters.label: Fill
parameters.description: fill - Overlay layer is stretched/squashed to fill the resolution and aspect ratio of the Fixed Layer. fithorz - Overlay layer is stretched/squashed to fit the Fixed Layer horizontally. fitvert - Overlay layer is stretched/squashed to fit the Fixed Layer vertically. fitbest - Overlay layer is stretch/squashed to fit the Fixed Layer using the best possible match that does not crop any of the Overlay layer. The aspect ratio of the Overlay is maintained. fitoutside - Overlay layer is stretched squashed to fit the Fixed Layer using the worst possible match. This is the opposite of Best Fit. The aspect ratio of the Overlay is maintained. nativeres - Overlay is not squashed or stretched. The Overlay layer uses its own resolution and aspect ratio during the composite. Pixel accurate composites require Native , use this setting for to maintain an image's original resolution during the composite.

parameters.name: Justify Horizontal
parameters.label: Justify Horizontal
parameters.description:
justifyh -  - Specify the horizontal alignment of the Overlay.

 left - The Overlay is aligned to the left side of the Fixed Layer. center - The Overlay is centered in the Fixed Layer. right - The Overlay is aligned to the right side of the Fixed Layer.

parameters.name: Left
parameters.label: Left
parameters.description: left - The Overlay is aligned to the left side of the Fixed Layer. center - The Overlay is centered in the Fixed Layer. right - The Overlay is aligned to the right side of the Fixed Layer.

parameters.name: Justify Vertical
parameters.label: Justify Vertical
parameters.description:
justifyv -  - Specify the vertical alignment of the Overlay.

 bottom - The Overlay is aligned to the bottom of the Fixed Layer. center - The Overlay is centered in the Fixed Layer. top - The Overlay is aligned to the top of the Fixed Layer.

parameters.name: Bottom
parameters.label: Bottom
parameters.description: bottom - The Overlay is aligned to the bottom of the Fixed Layer. center - The Overlay is centered in the Fixed Layer. top - The Overlay is aligned to the top of the Fixed Layer.

parameters.name: Extend Overlay
parameters.label: Extend Overlay
parameters.description:
extend -  - Sets the extend (or repeat) conditions of the Overlay layer. This parameter determines what happens at the edges of the Overlay layer.

 hold - The pixel values at the edges of the Overlay layer continue to extend past that edge. zero - The image does not extend past the edges of the Overlay. repeat - The image is repeated at the edges of the Overlay. mirror - The image is mirrored at the edges of the Overlay.

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.label: Hold
parameters.description: hold - The pixel values at the edges of the Overlay layer continue to extend past that edge. zero - The image does not extend past the edges of the Overlay. repeat - The image is repeated at the edges of the Overlay. mirror - The image is mirrored at the edges of the Overlay.

parameters.name: Rotate
parameters.label: Rotate
parameters.description: r - Rotates the Overlay layer. Increasing values rotate clockwise, decreasing values rotate counter-clockwise.

parameters.name: Translate
parameters.label: Translate
parameters.description:
t -  - Translates the Overlay layer in x and y.

 tx - ty -

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: tx - ty -

parameters.name: Translate Units
parameters.label: Translate Units
parameters.description: tunit - Sets the units used in the Translate parameter.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
s -  - Scales the Overlay layer in x and y.

 sx - sy -

parameters.name: Pivot
parameters.label: Pivot
parameters.description:
p -  - Allows you to define the point about which the Overlay layer scales and rotates. Altering the pivot point produces different results depending on the Transform Order.

 px - py -

parameters.name: Pivot Units
parameters.label: Pivot Units
parameters.description: punit - Sets the units used in the Pivot parameter.

parameters.name: Translate Step
parameters.label: Translate Step
parameters.description:
tstep -  - Translates the Overlay layers cumulatively, starting at the 3rd input (index 2). That layer is translated by the amount specified, the next layer is translated by 2 times that amount, the following layer by 3 times that amount and so on.

 tstepx - tstepy -

parameters.name: Translate Step Units
parameters.label: Translate Step Units
parameters.description: tstepunit - Sets the units used in the Translate Step parameter

parameters.name: Legacy Transform
parameters.label: Legacy Transform
parameters.description: legacyxform - When enabled, will use the legacy method of building the transform matrix, which has inverted rotation and transform order.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. Making this larger than 1 is essentially the same as taking the output from each pass, and passing it into the first input of the node and repeating the process. Other inputs and parameters remain the same for each pass.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.

pythonExamples.title: Composite Multiple Layers
pythonExamples.code:
# Configure Composite TOP
comp = op('comp1')
comp.par.operand = 'over'  # Alpha compositing
# Also: 'add', 'multiply', 'screen', 'subtract'
